summary: First test run for the Emergent Autonomous Coding Platform. Backend: 12/18 pytest cases pass on a clean backend. Frontend: ProjectList, Settings dialog, template buttons, Workspace (timeline + chat + preview + status bar) all render and function correctly. WebSocket /api/ws/{id} verified working (hello + ping->pong). Full 4-agent pipeline (scout->architect->coder->reviewer) completed end-to-end on TEST_counter and produced a working dark-mode counter app rendered in the live preview iframe. Iteration messages, /preview HTML, /finalize (mock), /settings round-trip all work. The 6 failing tests are TIMEOUTS caused by the FastAPI event loop being blocked while a pipeline is running concurrently (root cause: sync LLM calls inside emergentintegrations.LlmChat). When tests are run individually all 18 pass.

critical.issue: Pipeline LLM calls block the FastAPI event loop. While a build is running every other API request (including GET /api/, GET /api/projects/{id}) times out (>15s) because litellm.completion is invoked synchronously inside emergentintegrations.LlmChat.send_message. This is reproducible: 1) POST /api/projects, 2) within ~3s any unrelated GET /api/... hangs >30s. Backend appears 'frozen' to clients and to its own WebSocket pongs.

No retry / backoff on transient LLM 502 errors. Backend logs show 'litellm.BadGatewayError: Error code: 502' from gemini/anthropic, which causes the entire build to go to status='failed'. A single retry (or 2-attempt with jitter) would make the pipeline far more robust.

asyncio.create_task(_launch_pipeline(...)) has no concurrency cap. Multiple concurrent project creations (or rapid retries) launch unbounded pipelines that compete for LLM calls and crush the event loop. Add a Semaphore (e.g. 2) or a per-project queue.

minor.issue: Deprecated FastAPI startup/shutdown event API. Use lifespan context manager.

Server sends a string 'pong' (ws.send_text) but every other message is JSON. Frontend code that JSON.parses every message will throw on 'pong'. Either send {type:'pong'} or document/skip non-JSON frames in the client.

Returns 409 only if project.status=='running' at the moment of the call, but POST /api/projects sets status to 'queued' synchronously and the orchestrator flips to 'running' a moment later — there is a race window where a follow-up iteration POST could slip through and run a second pipeline concurrently.

integration_issues.issue: On initial mount of Workspace.jsx the WS is briefly closed before opening (CONSOLE: 'WebSocket connection ... failed: WebSocket is closed before the connection is established'). After the second open, status pill shows 'ws: connected' correctly, so it resolves itself. Likely a useEffect cleanup race or dev-mode StrictMode double-invoke. Not a reconnect-loop, but worth a look.

issues: Radix DialogContent missing aria-describedby (a11y warning in console). Add <DialogDescription> or aria-describedby for screen-reader users.

action_items: CRITICAL: Wrap the sync litellm call in asyncio.to_thread (or a real async client) so the FastAPI event loop is not blocked during a 30-90s LLM build. Until then the backend is single-tenant: only one user can use it while a build is running.
Add 1-2 retries with exponential backoff for upstream LLM 502/503 errors in GenXProvider.complete or in Orchestrator._run_agent.
Cap concurrent pipelines with an asyncio.Semaphore in server._launch_pipeline.
Send WebSocket ping reply as JSON ({'type':'pong'}) for consistency.
Wrap in lifespan context manager instead of @app.on_event('shutdown').
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:151 / agents/genx_provider.py:71 — sync LLM call inside async function blocks event loop, the single biggest issue in this MVP. Use asyncio.to_thread(litellm.completion, ...) inside emergentintegrations OR run the orchestrator in an executor.
orchestrator.py — no try/retry around self.provider.complete; one upstream 502 fails the entire build with no recovery.
server.py — no concurrency cap on pipelines; create_task fired-and-forgotten with no tracking.
server.py:286 — POST /api/settings writes os.environ and rewrites .env even for empty strings; that is fine but consider explicit clear for empty values.
preview.py renders inlined HTML — verified working but X-Frame-Options is set to SAMEORIGIN which is correct for the iframe inside the same origin, good.
seed_data_creation: Two projects created in MongoDB during testing (TEST_counter, E2E Test). TEST_counter has a complete 4-agent build and is suitable for further inspection. They can be deleted via DELETE /api/projects/{id} or via mongo.
context_for_next_testing_agent: 1) Restart backend before any test run (sudo supervisorctl restart backend) and clean stale 'running' projects with: mongosh emergent_platform --eval 'db.projects.deleteMany({status: {\$in:["running","queued","failed"]}})'. 2) Run pytest with --asyncio-mode=auto. 3) The full pipeline test takes ~90-180s and depends on upstream LLM availability — be tolerant of occasional 502s. 4) The full backend_test.py suite will SELF-CONGEST because it creates several projects whose pipelines run in parallel — to retest after the event-loop-blocking fix, run only the slow tests one at a time. 5) Frontend public URL = https://coder-sandbox.preview.emergentagent.com (see /app/frontend/.env). 6) data-testids verified working: header-settings-btn, create-project-btn, template-*, project-row-*, agent-timeline, agent-row-{scout,architect,coder,reviewer}, chat-panel, chat-input-textarea, chat-send-btn, tab-preview, tab-code, live-preview, preview-iframe, status-bar, status-pill-ready, header-repo-link.
rca of the issue: When an orchestrator pipeline is running, every unrelated API call to the backend times out (>15s read timeout). Reproduction: (a) POST /api/projects with any prompt; (b) within 1-2 seconds run curl --max-time 10 https://.../api/  -> times out at 10s (no response). Root cause: emergentintegrations.LlmChat._execute_completion calls litellm.completion synchronously (proven by the stack trace in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log: 'await self._execute_completion' -> 'litellm.completion(**params)' which is a blocking call). Because the orchestrator awaits this from the same uvicorn worker event loop, the loop is blocked for the full duration of the LLM HTTP round trip (and during retries). Mitigation in app code (don't need to change emergentintegrations): in GenXProvider.complete, wrap the call as `text = await asyncio.to_thread(lambda: asyncio.run(chat.send_message(UserMessage(text=user_message))))` OR run the entire orchestrator in a process-pool executor. Cleanest fix: add a Semaphore + run_in_executor in server._launch_pipeline.
